📚 Doc reconciliation — where the numbers drifted, and why

PR #3040. Every correction below was verified against the repo or its own validators. The interesting finding is not any single wrong number — it is why one of them could stay wrong indefinitely while every automated check passed.

The stamper blind spot

One doc claimed 143 global hooks across 27 event types. Actual: 150 across 29. It had been drifting silently, and it is the copy that ships to users.

stamp-counts.sh rewritesthe "N global hook entries" phrase
…under docs/site/✓ stamped → doctor.mdx correctly said 150
…under src/skills/**✗ NEVER stamped → source drifted to 143
npm run build copies src → plugins/✗ the 143 ships to users
validate-counts.shpasses — it does not check this phrase

So the generated artifact was right while the source was wrong — the inverse of the usual drift direction, which is why no one caught it. #3026 widened a pre-existing gap rather than creating it.

Corrections

surfacewas → nowwhy it mattered

Left alone, deliberately

Not everything that looks stale is stale. This repo's drift audits historically run ~60% false-positive, so each of these was checked and rejected.

CHANGELOG.md "12 bundles"          frozen history — correct as written
README.md v7.30.0 highlights block  frozen release note, not a live claim
plugins/**                          generated; regenerates from src/ on build
hooks.json vs count-hooks.sh        the two sources of truth AGREE at this
                                    HEAD (150/45/22/217) — nothing to fix
